free_text: Excitation Functions for Helion-Induced Reactions in
particles were used for the bombardments.
Commercially available natural iron foil 99.9% pure
was used for target stacks. Thicknesses 10, 100, and
Stacks of iron foils were exposed to
helion beams. Beginning a few minutes after each
bombardment and at intervals during the following
month, gamma-ray spectra were measured with a Ge(Li)
spectrometer which was calibrated for both energy and
intensity measurements. The spectra were recorded by
a multichannel analyzer and transferred to a disc
storage unit of a digital computer. The spectra and
data lists were used to determine the count rate for
individual peaks. Radionuclides were identified by
Continuous curves, points were added by a compiler.
